spec.goal: Implement XY-165 by normalizing retained retry recovery so Maestro treats the historical full project slug and the trailing Linear slugId as the same self-dogfood project when rebuilding retained lanes.
success_criteria: Retained retry dispatch accepts an In Progress issue when the configured tracker project uses the historical full slug but the refreshed issue payload carries only the trailing Linear slugId for that same project.
Retry dispatch still rejects issues that belong to a genuinely different project.
Recovery, workflow contract docs, and pilot guidance describe the same project-slug compatibility rule.
Repo-native verification passes after the recovery normalization change.
constraints: Keep config-versus-WORKFLOW contract validation strict; only normalize the recovered issue-to-config project comparison in retry recovery.
Do not widen scope into changing Linear project lookup inputs or redesigning broader tracker identity routing.
Preserve rejection of unrelated projects and existing retry guard behavior.

tasks.title: Define the retained-retry project identity boundary
tasks.objective: Constrain the fix to the recovered retry project-match gate so Maestro accepts the configured full slug and trailing slugId variants for the same Linear project without weakening unrelated-project rejection.
inputs: Current retry dispatch and recovery code in src/orchestrator.rs
outputs: A concrete compatibility rule for same-project slug matching in retained retry recovery
A clear non-goal boundary for tracker config validation and unrelated project rejection
verification: The chosen rule explains why XY-156 was skipped despite a retained workspace
The rule leaves config-versus-WORKFLOW equality and unrelated-project rejection intact

tasks.title: Implement retry recovery normalization and regression coverage
tasks.objective: Patch the retry recovery path and tests so retained workspaces recover correctly across full-slug and slugId variants while still rejecting other projects.
inputs: Retained recovery and targeted retry tests
outputs: Runtime code that normalizes configured full slug versus issue payload slugId variants for retained retry matching
Regression tests for both acceptance of the same project and rejection of a different project
verification: Targeted retry dry-run coverage accepts the same project slugId variant
Recovered workspace selection coverage accepts the same project slugId variant
Existing unrelated-project retry rejection still passes

tasks.title: Align docs and verify the recovery fix
tasks.objective: Update the operator/spec docs for the compatibility rule and verify the repo stays green under the standard Maestro checks.
outputs: Docs that explain the full-slug versus slugId compatibility rule consistently
Verification evidence for fmt, lint, and test gates
verification: Docs use the same compatibility rule as the runtime change
Run the full defaults.verification_commands set successfully

triggers: The fix requires changing canonical tracker config inputs rather than only retained retry comparison behavior
Linear project identity proves inconsistent across more than the full-slug versus trailing slugId forms captured here
Recovery correctness depends on a broader orchestrator redesign outside retained retry matching

evidence: 2026-03-18: Retained retry recovery was skipping XY-156 because the configured self-dogfood project slug used the historical full slug while refreshed Linear issue payloads reported only the trailing slugId variant for the same project.
2026-03-18: src/orchestrator.rs now normalizes the configured full project slug against the trailing Linear slugId when matching retained retry issues, while still rejecting unrelated projects.

2026-03-18: Regression coverage now includes retry_run_dry_run_accepts_issue_slug_id_variant_of_configured_project and run_project_once_recovers_retained_workspace_for_issue_slug_id_variant alongside the existing different-project rejection test.
2026-03-18: docs/guide/pilot.md, docs/spec/system_workflow_contract.md, and docs/spec/system_maestro_runtime.md now describe the same full-slug versus slugId compatibility rule.
2026-03-18: Local verification passed for cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, and cargo make test.

context_snapshot.resolved_gap: Retained retry recovery must accept the historical full self-dogfood slug and the trailing Linear slugId as the same project.
